channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Boeing Shifts Focus to Defense Amid Civil Aviation Challenges
item.description: Boeing, long recognized as a leader in commercial aviation, is increasingly relying on its defense division as civil aviation faces significant challenges. According to Reuters, the company recently s...

item.title: Venezuelan Activist Lilian Tintori Discusses Resilience Amid Political Persecution
item.description: Venezuelan democracy activist Lilian Tintori, along with her husband Leopoldo Lopez, has faced ongoing political persecution despite the reported capture of President Maduro by U.S. forces. Tintori ex...

item.title: CTV News observes Ottawa commuters’ daily OC Transpo routines
item.description: CTV News followed Ottawa residents during their daily commute on OC Transpo, highlighting the complex decisions commuters face every morning. From choosing the fastest bus route to balancing transfers...

item.title: Multiple fatalities and injuries in Swiss bus fire in Kerzers
item.description: A tragic incident occurred on Tuesday evening in the town of Kerzers, western Switzerland, when a coach carrying passengers caught fire around 6:25pm. According to police reports, the blaze was allege...

item.title: Tanzanian Government and Dangote Cement Discuss Cooperation to Boost Industrial Growth
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, the Minister of Finance, Mr Khamis Mussa Omar, met with a delegation from Dangote Cement Tanzania led by its Chief Executive Officer, Mr Adeyemi Fajobi, to deliberate on ways to enha...

item.title: Economic and Political Beneficiaries of the Iran Conflict
item.description: The recent airstrikes on Iran by US and Israeli forces have sent shockwaves through global markets, with immediate and far-reaching economic consequences. The UK faces particular challenges, as GDP gr...

item.title: Government Announces Sh2 Billion Compensation for Protest Victims and Establishes Coalition Taskforce
item.description: President William Ruto has announced a Sh2 billion fund to compensate victims of police brutality during protests and riots in Kenya. This comes as he and former Vice President Kalonzo Musyoka's ally ...

item.title: Trump and Putin discuss Ukraine, Iran conflicts and global oil concerns
item.description: On Monday, U.S. President Donald Trump spoke with Russian President Vladimir Putin over phone for the first time this year. The discussion mainly covered the war in Iran, peace prospects in Ukraine, a...

item.title: IndiGo CEO Pieter Elbers resigns early, Rahul Bhatia takes interim charge after December flight disruptions
item.description: IndiGo, India’s largest airline, announced the resignation of its CEO Pieter Elbers, who had been contracted until 2027. The resignation comes just three months after severe operational disruptions in...

item.title: South African gamers compete in 48-hour Apex Legends LAN tournament
item.description: Red Bull Locked In, a 48-hour Apex Legends LAN tournament, brought together four teams and 120 local gamers at the Bellville Velodrome in Cape Town. Each team was led by a well-known South African gam...

item.title: A Retrospective of Louis Theroux’s Most Notable Documentaries
item.description: Louis Theroux has spent nearly three decades creating documentaries that probe some of society’s most unusual and challenging subjects. From his early series, Weird Weekends, where he immersed himself...

item.title: Peter Mansbridge narrates ad for South Korean submarine company without disclosing prior work to Poilievre or listeners
item.description: Peter Mansbridge, the retired CBC News anchor, recently faced scrutiny for narrating a promotional advertisement for Hanwha Ocean, a South Korean firm competing to supply Canada with its next fleet of...

item.title: AI Tool Identifies Vulnerabilities in Decades-Old Apple II Program
item.description: Microsoft Azure CTO Mark Russinovich demonstrated that AI can now analyze decades-old machine code to uncover vulnerabilities, using his own Apple II program from 1986 as an example. The program, name...

item.title: IATA Report Highlights Aviation Safety Challenges Across Africa in 2025
item.description: The International Air Transport Association (IATA) don release their 2025 Annual Safety Report wey show say Africa still get the highest aviation accident rate for the world. Even though the number of...

item.title: The Physics Behind How Locomotives Pull Extremely Heavy Trains
item.description: A single locomotive can pull hundreds of fully loaded freight cars, totaling thousands of tons, thanks to the physics of friction. While it might seem impossible for a 150-ton engine to move a 10,000-...

item.title: Aga Khan Fund sells NPRT stake holding NMG shares to Tanzanian firm
item.description: The Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development (AKFED) has announced it is selling its 100 percent shareholding in NPRT Holdings Africa Limited to Taarifa Ltd, owned by Tanzanian businessman Rostam Aziz. ...

item.title: Three people killed and four injured in Kings Highway collision near Queanbeyan
item.description: A tragic collision occurred on Monday evening along the Kings Highway at Monga, roughly 80 kilometres east of Queanbeyan, involving a Hyundai sedan and a Hyundai hatchback. The crash happened around 5...

item.title: U.S. strikes inactive mine-laying vessels amid tensions with Iran over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States military reported the destruction of 10 inactive mine-laying vessels in response to potential Iranian activity in the Strait of Hormuz. President Donald Trump warned that any mines l...

item.title: Ongoing Tensions Between US and Iran Escalate, Including Threats and Regional Military Movements
item.description: The conflict between the United States and Iran has intensified, with a mix of military strikes, diplomatic warnings, and energy market disruptions. US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth announced what he...

item.title: Pakistan urges global action against Israeli military operations in Lebanon
item.description: Pakistan’s Foreign Office (FO) on Tuesday strongly condemned the recent escalation of Israeli military operations in Lebanon, calling upon the international community to take immediate action to halt ...
